game_name: Dune II: The Building of a Dynasty

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: First few missions focus on establishing unoccupied base territory
Core mechanic: harvester vehicles collect spice, refinery converts to credits
Build military units with credits to defend against enemy incursions
Gigantic sandworm swallows vehicles/infantry; blocked by rocky terrain only
Terrain bumps become spice fields when shot/run over; unit destroyed in spice blow
Higher missions unlock improved tech & unique house weaponry
Harkonnen Devastator, Atreides Sonic Tank, Ordos Deviator unlock at different mission tiers
All missions after first two require destroying enemy bases; can capture enemy Factory to build their units
Two enemy factions form temporary alliance; final battle includes Emperor's Sardaukar elite forces

typical_relief_triggers: Clearing fog of war via exploration
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating difficulty curve across 9-territory campaign; mission times increase from 2-3 minutes (Mission 1) to 8-30 minutes (Mission 9); tension builds through progressive enemy aggression and endgame alliance

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Orange spice field coloration; darker orange = higher concentration; spice blooms appear as terrain bumps
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Spice blooms hidden as terrain features; Rocky terrain blocks sandworms and enables building; concrete foundations reduce repair costs but don't prevent decay

difficulty_curve.early_game: Missions 1-3: Base establishment in unoccupied territory; focus on spice harvesting and basic defense; minimal enemy pressure (Atreides 2-6 min, Harkonnen 2-3.5 min)
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Missions 4-6: Increasing enemy base confrontation; technology unlocks enable house-specific units; mission times extend to 6-9 minutes; enemy territories become contested
difficulty_curve.late_game: Missions 7-9: Direct enemy base destruction required; specialized units deployed; Sardaukar elite forces introduced; endgame alliance forces final showdown; mission times jump to 7-40 minutes (significant variance)
notable_spikes: Mission 3→4 transition: First mandatory enemy base destruction
Mission 6→7: Introduction of specialized house units
Final endgame: Two-house alliance + Sardaukar forces creates complexity spike

notable_level_patterns.description: Player chooses next territory from 2-3 options on Arrakis map; choice determines enemy house faced; 9 territories total required for completion regardless of house selection
Completing higher missions grants authorization for improved technology and house-unique weaponry; Harkonnen get Devastator tanks, Atreides get Sonic Tanks, Ordos get Deviator nerve-gas vehicles
Harvesters require protection from sandworms and enemy units; spice fields appear in sand (orange coloration); hidden spice blooms reward exploration but punish contact
Map initially dark except unit vision range; exploration permanently lifts fog (unlike Warcraft II); encourages early scouting for resources and hazards
